summary: Merged the fighter/game-mode command-script state and library CommandInfo representations into one shared `CommandInfo` type under `lb/types.h`, then retargeted fighter and item command execution code to use it. The PR is primarily a cross-system type/header refactor: `FtCmdState` was removed from `gm/types.h`, fighter `ftCmdScript` storage became `CommandInfo`, command callbacks now take `CommandInfo*`, and command pointer/timer fields were renamed to typed names such as `u`, `timer`, `frame_count`, and `loop_count`. Evidence is limited by an empty PR body and no human review comments; the only comment is a decomp.dev report showing multiple unmatched-function regressions after the merge.
what_changed: `CommandInfo` was expanded into the shared command-script state in `src/melee/lb/types.h`, including `timer`, `frame_count`, a `union CmdUnion* u`, `loop_count`, `event_return`, `loop_count_dup`, and `unk_x18`.
The old fighter/game-mode command state `struct FtCmdState` was deleted from `src/melee/gm/types.h`.
The old fighter-local `struct ftCmdScript` was deleted from `src/melee/ft/types.h`; `Fighter::x3E4_fighterCmdScript` now has type `CommandInfo`.

`src/melee/ft/forward.h` now includes `lb/forward.h`, removes forward declarations for `ftCmdScript` and `FtCmdState`, and changes `typedef void (*FtCmd)(Fighter_GObj* gobj, FtCmdState* cmd)` to `CommandInfo*`.
`src/melee/lb/forward.h` gained `typedef struct CommandInfo CommandInfo;` so fighter/item headers can refer to the shared type without owning its definition.
`lbcommand.h` stopped defining a separate `_CommandInfo` and now includes `lb/forward.h`; the full definition lives in `lb/types.h`.
`gm/inlines.h` was renamed/moved to `lb/inlines.h`, keeping command pointer helpers such as `SKIP_CMD` in the library layer instead of the game-mode layer.
`lbcommand.c` was rewritten from raw `u32* data_position` access to typed `info->u->Command_##` bitfield access and `NEXT_CMD(info)` advancement.

Fighter script initialization in `fighter.c` and `ftwaitanim.c` now assigns `x3E4_fighterCmdScript.u = (union CmdUnion*) anim_script_ptr`, resets `loop_count`, and uses `timer` instead of old `x3E4_scriptEventTimer`.

Large portions of `ftaction.c` were retargeted from `FtCmdState*` to `CommandInfo*`, including callback prototypes, command dispatch tables, timer/frame loop logic, and many command handlers using `cmd->u->...` fields.
Item command-processing files such as `it_2725.c`, `it_2725.h`, `item.c`, and item forward declarations were adjusted to accept/use `CommandInfo*` for item scripts as well.
smart_moves: Centralizing `CommandInfo` in `lb/types.h` reflects that command scripts are shared infrastructure used by fighter, item, and library code rather than a `gm`-owned or fighter-local type.
Using `lb/forward.h` for `CommandInfo` lets public fighter/item forward headers depend on a forward declaration instead of pulling in the full bitfield-heavy struct everywhere.

Replacing anonymous/raw script pointer reads with `union CmdUnion` bitfield views makes individual opcodes searchable by names such as `Command_03`, `set_hitbox_damage`, `set_hurt_state`, and `damage_amount`.

The rename from offset-style fields (`x3E4_scriptEventTimer`, `x3E8_scriptFrameTimer`, `x3EC_scriptCurrent`, `x3F0_scriptLoopNum`) to semantic shared names (`timer`, `frame_count`, `u`, `loop_count`) makes subsequent command interpreter work easier to grep and reason about.
Keeping `SKIP_CMD`/`NEXT_CMD` style pointer advancement isolates command-stream pointer arithmetic from the many individual action handlers.
decomp_lessons: When two subsystems are independently modeling the same binary command state, prefer one shared struct in a neutral/library header rather than duplicate structs in subsystem headers.
For command bytecode interpreters, a union of opcode-specific bitfield layouts can document known encodings while still allowing unknown layouts (`unk##`) to coexist until named.
Moving a struct can improve type hygiene but may perturb matching heavily; here decomp.dev reported regressions in 14 unmatched functions after the type merge.
Header ownership matters: command script parsing helpers were moved from `gm/inlines.h` to `lb/inlines.h`, matching their broader library use.
Changing field names across a large interpreter should be done with careful build/match validation because even semantically equivalent pointer-expression rewrites can affect generated code.

A shared `CommandInfo` used by fighter and item code should preserve layout-sensitive padding/aliases; this PR kept comments like `ptr[1]` as a matching hack for `Command_04` and noted that `event_return[3]` size was made-up.
naming_conventions: `CommandInfo` is the shared command-script state type.
`CmdUnion` is the shared union for opcode-specific command word interpretations.
Generic command opcodes are named `Command_00`, `Command_01`, etc., with union members such as `Command_03` and `Command_09`.
Known opcode payloads receive semantic member names when understood, e.g. `set_hitbox_damage`, `set_hitbox_scale`, `set_cmd_var`, `set_hurt_state`, and `damage_amount`.
Unknown command payloads remain numbered as `unk0`, `unk1`, `unk22`, etc., often with comments pointing to the function that uses them.
The command stream pointer is now named `u`; timer/frame bookkeeping is named `timer` and `frame_count`; nesting state is named `loop_count` and `event_return`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `NEXT_CMD(info)` and `SKIP_CMD(cmd, n)` are used to advance `CommandInfo::u` through the command stream instead of manually updating raw `u32* data_position` everywhere.

Loop/subroutine/goto commands in `lbcommand.c` now manipulate `union CmdUnion*` pointers: `event_return[loop_count++] = info->u + 1`, `info->u = info->u->Command_05.ptr`, and `info->u = info->u->Command_07.ptr`.
`Command_04` keeps an alias `u32* ptr[1]` in the `CommandInfo` union with a comment that it is a hack to match `Command_04`.
Fighter command execution preserves the `F32_MAX` timer sentinel behavior while renaming fields to `timer` and `frame_count`.
Multi-word command handling can require manual pointer increments, shown by `ftAction_80071E04` using `++cmd->u` before reading subsequent `unk26_1` / `unk26_2` words.
The diff includes some commented-out rewrites and temporary-looking code in `ftaction.c`; paired with the bot's regression report, this suggests the type unification was not purely match-neutral.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
The PR body was empty, so intent is inferred from the title and diff.

decomp.dev bot reported 14 regressions in unmatched functions for GALE01, including `ftAction_8007121C`, `ftAction_80071FC8`, `ftAction_80072320`, `ftAction_80071028`, `ftAction_80072E4C`, `ftAction_80071B50`, `ftAction_80072CD8`, and item functions in `it_2725`.

The bot report specifically showed several functions dropping from high partial match percentages to near-zero after the change, so future follow-up likely needed rematching after the structural cleanup.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching `CommandInfo`, `CmdUnion`, or `lb/types.h` to see how this shared command struct was renamed or stabilized.
Find follow-up PRs that recover matches for `ftAction_80071028`, `ftAction_8007121C`, `ftAction_80071FC8`, `ftAction_80072320`, or `it_802799E4` after this merge.
Search for remaining references to `FtCmdState`, `ftCmdScript`, `x3EC_scriptCurrent`, and `x3E4_scriptEventTimer` to verify the old command state names were fully retired.
Inspect later changes to `NEXT_CMD`, `SKIP_CMD`, and `Command_04` for better matching tactics around `CommandInfo::u` pointer arithmetic.
Search for union members named `unk##` in `CmdUnion` that later gained semantic names.
